entry.title: Re: Reactivating Archived Equipment / Asset – Is it possi...
entry.content:
First step would be AOBJ > is there a reload program for your version/system?

If none, consider creation of such tool with the Archive Development Kit / reload program ADK

entry.title: Work Zone search tool scope: default to "All" all the time?
entry.content:
Is it possible to configure the search tool in Work Zone such that the default scope setting is "All", regardless of whether the user is in a workspace, viewing a workpage or any other situation?

We're using WZ as an intranet and we're using workspaces to accommodate our top-level sections. However, we don't want to expose users to the actual concept of a "Workspace" - it doesn't make sense as a term.

But the search scope defaults to "This Workspace" when you're in a workspace. We want it to show  "All" regardless of where the user is.

TBH it's a dumb decision to constrain search to only the current workspace by default. The only consequence is to hide possibly useful results just because they happen to be in other workspaces. 

How can I configure the search tool to show "All" by default?

entry.title: My Inbox Showing Blank
entry.content:
Hi,

I am configuring Flexible workflow for Purchase Orders in On-Prem system and I do find that the content does not show: 

Any Helpful tips you may provide please?

entry.title: SAP Public Cloud - Purchase Order Adobe Form
entry.content:
Dear All

We have a requirement to show Tax details by using Tax Code available at line item within Purchase Order but Tax Code is not mapped within Odata Services used in Adobe Form.

FDP_EF_PURCHASE_ORDER_GLO_MENA_SRV
FDP_EF_PURCHASE_ORDER_SRV

Any idea how to include remaining standard fields within standard Odata Services or any option to create a custom Odata Service by copying the standard one.

Hi @Rakesh_Kr , yes, Fiori Tracker has the User usage report. See the details here:

https://fiori-tracker.github.io/features/user-usage-report/

Regards
Greg Malewski
Nype

entry.content: Thank you Ryota

entry.title: Request for Guidance on Activating Standard Retail Merchandise Management Roles
entry.content:
Hello,

The partner contact for my customer is seeking advice regarding standard roles and authorization objects for SAP S/4HANA Retail for Merchandise Management (SAP S/4HANA Cld for Ret merch, priv ed 8008751). The customer, is aiming to use purely retail management roles without triggering Advanced FUE classifications. Currently, some objects in their manual configuration are causing tech engine users to be classified as advanced, and the goal is to create pure retail roles without Advanced FUE by excluding advanced authorization objects.

They referred to the standard roles listed in the SAP help documentation for SAP S/4HANA Retail for merchandise management. However, these roles are not appearing in the system as listed in the guide as per the customer. The partner mentioned that during implementation, roles were activated via Rapid Activation. Their suggestion was to activate the standard roles mentioned in the guide using Rapid Activation, then assign them to a test user to verify that they do not require an advanced user or tech classification and are sufficient for daily tasks.

Could anyone share insights on the following?

Is Rapid Activation the recommended method to activate these retail module standard roles?

What are the best practices to ensure that the activated roles avoid advanced FUE classifications?

Are there additional checks or adjustments needed after activation to guarantee they meet the daily operational requirements for the retail module?

What is the recommended approach to filter out Advanced FUE objects when creating custom roles via Rapid Activation?

Thanks in advance for your guidance.

entry.title: Re: PM-order: change field value in operations
entry.content: hi i want to change the field values of order of t-code iw32 is there any bapi or fm to change ?

entry.title: Re: Flexible workflow PR approval with HR mini master
entry.content: Thank you for your feedback

entry.title: Re: Iterator Design Pattern in OOABAP
entry.content: Great write-up!  Recently I was examining something similar to an iterator model, which SAP provides, which is the IF_ABAP_READER interface, which seems to be loosely modelled on a Java InputStream.  It has a DATA_AVAILABLE() method to see if there is data available (similar to HAS_NEXT()) and a READ() method to read the next data (similar to GET_NEXT()) plus a few methods that are more stream-specific like skipping data, closing a stream etc.  There are some sub-interfaces like IF_ABAP_ITAB_READER and IF_ABAP_DB_READER which provide their own READ() methods which are more strongly typed.  It would be nice if SAP adopted IF_ABAP_READER as a standard iterator interface, but I was surprised to find that IF_ABAP_READER does not have a C1 contract in BTP ABAP so I have the feeling SAP is not investing further in the stream paradigm.

entry.title: Re: HCM Brasil: Crédito do Trabalhador - eConsignado
entry.content:
Olá Leandro, bom dia.

Quando você fala sobre solução standard, a sap vai criar um infotype para fazer o controle dos dados a serem enviados para o Governo ?

Hoje a SAP já deixou uma badi disponível para preenchimento dos dados, entendo que seria uma contingência caso a solução não seja entregue a tempo, este pensamento está correto ?

Att,

Nei Rodrigues

entry.title: Re: SAP CALM API returns forbidden / 403 (in postman)
entry.content: Hi Friscas, Please provide the plain text of the JSON you used to update the Service Instance with, so I can verify it is complete.

entry.title: Re: SAP CALM API returns forbidden / 403 (in postman)
entry.content: Hi @john_p_grimes, { "xs-security": { "xsappname": "calmapi", "authorities": [ "$XSMASTERAPPNAME.calm-api.analytics.providers.read", "$XSMASTERAPPNAME.calm-api.analytics.read", "$XSMASTERAPPNAME.calm-api.bm.read", "$XSMASTERAPPNAME.calm-api.bsm.read", "$XSMASTERAPPNAME.calm-api.bsm.write", "$XSMASTERAPPNAME.calm-api.csa.read", "$XSMASTERAPPNAME.calm-api.defects.read", "$XSMASTERAPPNAME.calm-api.demo-tasks.read", "$XSMASTERAPPNAME.calm-api.documents.read", "$XSMASTERAPPNAME.calm-api.documents.write", "$XSMASTERAPPNAME.calm-api.event-situations.write", "$XSMASTERAPPNAME.calm-api.exm.personal.read", "$XSMASTERAPPNAME.calm-api.exm.read", "$XSMASTERAPPNAME.calm-api.exm.write", "$XSMASTERAPPNAME.calm-api.feature.personal.read", "$XSMASTERAPPNAME.calm-api.feature.read", "$XSMASTERAPPNAME.calm-api.hm.read", "$XSMASTERAPPNAME.calm-api.hm.write", "$XSMASTERAPPNAME.calm-api.iep.read", "$XSMASTERAPPNAME.calm-api.integration-monitoring.personal.read", "$XSMASTERAPPNAME.calm-api.integration-monitoring.read", "$XSMASTERAPPNAME.calm-api.integration-monitoring.write", "$XSMASTERAPPNAME.calm-api.jobs.read", "$XSMASTERAPPNAME.calm-api.landscape.read", "$XSMASTERAPPNAME.calm-api.logs.read", "$XSMASTERAPPNAME.calm-api.logs.write", "$XSMASTERAPPNAME.calm-api.metrics.read", "$XSMASTERAPPNAME.calm-api.metrics.write", "$XSMASTERAPPNAME.calm-api.processauthoring.delete", "$XSMASTERAPPNAME.calm-api.processauthoring.read", "$XSMASTERAPPNAME.calm-api.processauthoring.write", "$XSMASTERAPPNAME.calm-api.processmanagement.delete", "$XSMASTERAPPNAME.calm-api.processmanagement.read", "$XSMASTERAPPNAME.calm-api.processmanagement.write", "$XSMASTERAPPNAME.calm-api.projects.read", "$XSMASTERAPPNAME.calm-api.projects.write", "$XSMASTERAPPNAME.calm-api.requirements.read", "$XSMASTERAPPNAME.calm-api.rum.read", "$XSMASTERAPPNAME.calm-api.rum.write", "$XSMASTERAPPNAME.calm-api.sum.read", "$XSMASTERAPPNAME.calm-api.sum.write", "$XSMASTERAPPNAME.calm-api.tasks.read", "$XSMASTERAPPNAME.calm-api.tasks.write", "$XSMASTERAPPNAME.calm-api.tests.personal.read", "$XSMASTERAPPNAME.calm-api.tests.read", "$XSMASTERAPPNAME.calm-api.traces.read", "$XSMASTERAPPNAME.calm-api.traces.write" ], "oauth2-configuration": { "credential-types": [ "binding-secret" ] } } }

entry.title: Rating ends with error which indicate wrong Charge was considered by CC
entry.content:
Hello Guys,

I am facing an issue for which cannot find an answer. I hope you will help me understand what I have done wrong that CC behavior is as it is.

What I am trying to do ?

Trying to test if chargé plan  designed for usage consumption works as expected.

What I did ?

  1. Created CCM for Subscription products with:
  • separate Charge Plan dedicated for Recurring Chargé triggered from CC using Variant Conditions
  • separate Charge Plan dedicated for Usage Chargé triggered when usage event arrives
  1. Created Subscription Contract which was created in CC correctly.
  2. Created CIT via FKKBIXCIT_UPLOAD – Upload and perform Rating for it.

Issue:

During Rating system throws error that rating failed. In the message system displays parameters which belongs to Recurring Charge instead of Usage Charge which suggests that CC ignore Charge dedicated for Usage, instead CC use Recurring Charge for some reason .

 

When I removed Recurring Charge Plan from the Subscription Contract in CC, for which I created CIT and performed rating again system rated CIT successfully!!

Does anybody knows what I have done wrong that system takes Recurring Charge instead Usage Charge when rating CIT ?

Thanks For help!

BR/Dominic

entry.title: Re: Which is the new version of C_HCADM_05 certification?
entry.content: @Alterman, As SAP Learning SAP HANA Portfolio Manager I can tell you that the C_DBADM certification will not be dead ended soon. You might have missed my last year’s blog on this topic: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/the-future-has-been-written-the-rise-of-the-database-administrator/ba-p/13713176. In the blog I explained that C_HANATEC and C_HCADM have been merged into one certification the C_DBADM. This new certification tests the cloud and on-premises knowledge of an SAP HANA database administrator.

entry.title: Job Information Manager Self-Service Now Displays Filtered Events Based on Event Reason Permissions
entry.content:
Job Information Manager Self-Service Now Displays Filtered Events Based on Event Reason Permissions

We're excited to share a new feature that significantly improves the user experience for managers using the "Change Job and Compensation Information" self-service transaction in Employee Central (EC). This enhancement addresses a highly voted feature request from our users (customer idea 48935), making the process more intuitive and efficient, particularly for customers who have set up their system to allow managers to manually select the Event and Event Reason.

The Challenge

Previously, managers faced a cluttered dropdown list of events, even if they didn't have permission for any of the associated event reasons. This often led to confusion and inefficiency when managers selected an Event and there was no single Event Reason available in the respective dropdown. In a nutshell, managers had to sift through irrelevant options.
Events displayed prior to the enhancement

The Solution

With our 1H 2025 update, we've introduced a filter to the Events dropdown list. Now, managers and admins will only see Events for which they have permission for at least one Event Reason which is active and valid for the selected date. This means that if a manager lacks permission for all event reasons tied to a specific event, that Event will no longer clutter their dropdown list. The same applies if there is no single active and valid Event Reason configured on the selected effective date.

Managers see Events only if they have permission for at least one associated Event Reason

How It Works

Let's break it down with an example:

Event Reasons Configured

There are many different Event Reasons configured in the system, usually several for each Event.

  • Data Change - Local Job Title (associated with Event Data Change)
  • Promotion - Pay Change (associated with Event Promotion)
  • Promotion - No Pay Change (associated with Event Promotion)
  • Transfer (associated with Event Transfer)
  • …

Some Event Reasons set up in the system

 

Manager's Permissions to Event Reasons

Managers typically have access to a limited set of Event Reasons, focusing on those essential for performing their duties effectively. Permissions control this access.

  • Data Change - Local Job Title
  • Promotion - No Pay Change 

Manager has permissions for 'Event Reasons Data Change - Local Job Title' and 'Promotion - No Pay Change' but does not have permissions for 'Promotion - Pay Change' and 'TRANSFER'

Events available in the Manager Self-Service Change Job and Compensation Information

When this manager accesses the Change Job and Compensation Information page, they will only see the following events in the dropdown:

  • Promotion (because they have permission for the Event Reason Promotion - No Pay Change, which is associated with this Event)
  • Data Change (because they have permission for the Event Reason Data Change - Local Job Title, which is associated with this Event)

Managers see Events only if they have permission for at least one associated Event Reason

 

Events like Transfer are excluded because the manager lacks permission for any associated Event Reason. This helps them focus on relevant options. This functionality is also mirrored in the Job Information History, ensuring consistency across the different UIs.

By optimizing the dropdown list, we are committed to elevating productivity and empowering managers to concentrate on their core responsibilities with greater efficiency. This enhancement is inspired by customer idea 48935 - Events should not appear if User does not have permissions to any associated event reasons.

 

Note: With the H1 2025 release, the new feature becomes available when managers first select Job Information in the transaction. However, if managers select Compensation Information before Job Information during this manager self-service transaction, they can view Events including those without an associated Event Reason for which they have permission. This gap is scheduled for a separate enhancement in a future release. For more details plese refer to https://me.sap.com/notes/0003586228. 

We welcome any questions or feedback; please leave a comment below this blog.

entry.title: Re: New SF BizX Instance Refresh Process Tool
entry.content:
Hi Vinay,

I hope you are doing well!

Please guide us on what exact backup should be taken for EC, integration from the target instance before the Instance refresh. 

Thanks,

KV

entry.title: Re: S/4Hana test & demo cloud
entry.content:
Hi @JuliaDoxee ,

For partners, we only provide a single TDD system (D tenant) without a test tenant. You use that for scenario demo/configuration like a Starter System. So far, I haven't heard about a 3 system landscape (D, T and P tenants) provisioned for a partner. For the development extensibility (adding new codes to D-080 tenant), the code you developed on your TDD system can be deposited to the ABAP GitHub, then upload it to your customer's D tenant for further deployment and testing. 

Since you are doing an add-on to the Public Cloud, would it make more sense to do a side-by-side extensibility, i.e., developing your solution on a BTP platform? 

Either way, you need to ask for a Partner Manager to guide you through.

Regards,

George

entry.title: SAP Fiori Tools 2503 Update: Icons, App Info Page, Adaptation Projects, Preview
entry.content:
The SAP Fiori tools team continues to enhance the developer experience with fresh updates aimed at modernizing the UI, improving usability, and increasing efficiency. In this 2503 release, we introduce a refreshed icon set, improvements to the Application Info page, a new approach to previewing applications using virtual endpoints, and enhancements for UI adaptation. Let’s take a closer look at what’s new in each of these areas; I’ll also include a list of some of the many continuous improvements in this release.

A Fresh Look: Modernized SAP Fiori Tools Extension Icons

To align with evolving SAP Fiori design standards, we gave the SAP Fiori tools extension icons a modern refresh. The updated icons are designed to better represent the purpose of each tool while making them more easily recognizable. This visual enhancement ensures that developers can quickly identify the tools they need while working within SAP Fiori tools.

Fresh icons for the tools in the SAP Fiori tools extension pack.

Enhanced Application Info Page for Better Usability

As the number of available actions for a given application has grown, navigating the Application Info page became increasingly challenging. To address this, we have redesigned the layout by grouping tiles into three distinct categories: Configuration, Common Tasks, and Manage. These groupings are based on usage data, helping developers find relevant actions more quickly. In addition, we moved description texts to tooltips, saving valuable screen space. We invite you to share your thoughts and suggestions for further improvements in the comments section.

New layout of the the Application Info page.

Preview Applications More Efficiently with Virtual Endpoints

Historically, SAP Fiori tools have relied on materialized HTML and JavaScript files to generate previews and test artifacts. However, maintaining these physical files posed challenges, particularly regarding updates and bug fixes, which required manual adjustments across multiple projects. With this update, we introduce virtual endpoints as part of the preview middleware. These endpoints dynamically generate code at runtime based on an application’s YAML configuration file. As a result, developers can simply update to the latest version of @sap/ux-ui5-tooling to receive improvements. To transition your application’s preview configuration to virtual endpoints, an action is available on the Application Info page. More details can be found here.

Making UI Adaptation More Accessible

SAP Fiori tools continues to simplify the process of adapting SAP-delivered applications with adaptation projects. One common challenge has been selecting the correct screen element for modification in a running application. To streamline this process, we have introduced adaptation functions within the context menu of the outline tree in the Adaptation Editor. This enhancement allows users to easily access and apply modifications. We are committed to further improve the work with adaptation projects and plan to expand the available actions in the future.

Access adaptation functions from the context menu in the outline panel.

 Continuous Improvements to Streamline Development

This release also brings several refinements and expanded capabilities across SAP Fiori tools:

  • The Project Accelerator (introduced in the 2501 release blog) now supports drag-and-drop images directly from the file explorer, making it easier to incorporate assets into projects.
  • The Adaptation Editor includes additional quick actions, such as enabling variant management for charts and tables, adding local annotation files, and enabling row creation for tables.
  • SAP Fiori Generator in SAP Build Code now leverages AI to intelligently determine input values when generating a UI from the Storyboard.
  • SAP Fiori tools now support the “component” project type, commonly used for SAP Fiori Launchpad plugins. The Application Info page is now available for component projects, providing relevant tools and insights.
  • Multi-service support in Service Manager and mock data configuration has been improved and fixed.
  • The SAP Fiori Generator’s “Basic” template now adheres to SAPUI5 1.120.x best practices.
  • Enhancements have been made to overview page-related guides (OVP) within Guided Development.
  • Page Editor now supports sorting for micro charts in the object header.

Looking Ahead: What’s Next?

We are constantly working on new features to further enhance SAP Fiori tools. Here’s a sneak peek at what’s coming next:

  • Completion of open-sourcing SAP Fiori generator modules.
  • Support for virtual endpoints at the generation stage, reducing setup complexity.
  • Introduction of LSP (Language Server Protocol) support for annotation files in adaptation projects.
  • Expansion of quick actions in the Adaptation Editor.
  • Support for Word document uploads in the Project Accelerator.
  • Continued investment in AI-powered features to boost productivity and make development more intuitive.

Stay tuned for more updates, and as always, we value your feedback. Let us know your thoughts in the comments below!

entry.title: Re: How to call a API in BADI implementation in HANA Clou...
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, calling an external API within a BADI implementation involves using the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) services, specifically the SAP Cloud SDK and HTTP client libraries. Here's how you can achieve this:

1. Choose the Right BADI

Identify the correct BADI using SAP Extensibility Explorer or SAP Help Portal.

Ensure the BADI allows external API calls (some are restricted in the public cloud).

2. Implement the BADI Using Custom Logic

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition supports custom logic via ABAP for Cloud Development. Follow these steps:

Step 1: Create the BADI Implementation

  1. Open SAP Fiori App → "Custom Logic".
  2. Find the required BADI (e.g., BADI_SALESORDER_CHANGE).
  3. Click Create Implementation.

Step 2: Use ABAP HTTP Client to Call the API

Since direct HTTP calls (e.g., cl_http_client) are restricted in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, use the SAP ABAP Environment HTTP Client:

TRY.

    DATA(lo_http_client) = cl_web_http_client=>create_by_url( 'https://api.example.com/data' ).

 

    " Set HTTP Method

    lo_http_client->request->set_method( if_web_http_request=>co_method_get ).

 

    " Set Headers (if required)

    lo_http_client->request->set_header( 'Content-Type', 'application/json' ).

    lo_http_client->request->set_header( 'Authorization', 'Bearer your_token_here' ).

 

    " Send Request

    lo_http_client->send( ).

 

    " Get Response

    DATA(lv_response) = lo_http_client->response->get_text( ).

 

    " Process Response

    WRITE: / 'API Response:', lv_response.

 

  CATCH cx_web_http_client_error INTO DATA(lo_error).

    WRITE: / 'HTTP Error:', lo_error->get_text( ).

ENDTRY.

3. Read Supplier Confirmation Data in ABAP

Within the BADI method, use ABAP logic to fetch supplier confirmation details. Example:

 

METHOD if_ex_mm_pur_confirmation_badi~post.

  

  DATA: lt_confirmations TYPE TABLE OF mm_pur_confirmation,

        ls_confirmation TYPE mm_pur_confirmation.

 

  " Read supplier confirmations

  lt_confirmations = io_confirmation->get_confirmations( ).

 

  " Loop through confirmations

  LOOP AT lt_confirmations INTO ls_confirmation.

    WRITE: / 'Supplier:', ls_confirmation-supplier,

           / 'PO:', ls_confirmation-purchase_order,

           / 'Confirmed Qty:', ls_confirmation-confirmed_quantity,

           / 'Delivery Date:', ls_confirmation-confirmed_delivery_date.

  ENDLOOP.

 

ENDMETHOD.

4. Deploy and Test

Save and Activate the BADI.

Test using Business Process Testing (BPT) in SAP Fiori.

 

I hope those elements will help you answering your question.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: Inactive Items Not Displayed in User Learning History When Using Search Bar

entry.title: Re: HCM Brasil: Crédito do Trabalhador - eConsignado
entry.content:
Bom dia @neirodrigues 

Isso mesmo, teremos um infotipo novo na solução, desconto na folha e eSocial.

A BAdI foi criada por uma contingência mesmo. Não sabíamos como seriam os prazos do governo já que o eSocial foi alterado já na versão S-1.2 para o eConsignado. Havia uma expectativa de entrada em produção ainda no ano passado. Também, caso o cliente/parceiro não queria usar o que iremos entregar, a BAdi permanecerá disponível.

Espero ter ajudado.

entry.title: Service broker error: Service broker destination-service-broker failed with: Failed to update the de
entry.content:
Hello All,

I developed a CAPM Fiori Application in BAS. I deployed the changes to DEV and CONS. Deployment is successful but i am having issue with destination update in BTP subaccount.

It's throwing below error saying that Update Failed:

Couldn't update service instance '<destination_service_instance_name>'. Service broker error: Service broker destination-service-broker failed with: Failed to update the destination service instance with the user provided configuration.

I tried to update the destination from BAS using mta.yaml configuration but i don't see any change in destination instance in BTP.

Kindly help me to resolve this issue.

Regards,

Mahendra

entry.title: Can I See Private Versions Created by Other Users with a Standard License?
entry.content:
Dear,

I have a question about access rights for different roles. In our system, we have three roles:

  • Admin (Professional License)

  • Premium Role (Standard License)

  • Planner Role (Standard License with fewer rights than Premium)

As an Admin, I can see if private versions have been created by users with Premium or Planner roles. However, I’d like to know if a user with a Premium Role (Standard License) can also see private versions created by other users (Premium or Planner), or if this visibility is limited only to Admins.

Has anyone found a way to enable such access?

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Is it possible to connect on Joule instance with SAP IBP and SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud
entry.content: Currently is it possible to connect same Joule instance with S/4HANA Public Cloud and SAP IBP system ?

Were you able to make it work? I'm trying to use I_SalesOrderTP BO but I'm not able to call COMMIT ENTITIES or CONVERT KEY sentences because of dumps. My system is a public cloud one so my hands are tied in a lof of ways. Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Dynamically filter the values displayed in an Input C...
entry.content: Thank you so much for your help!

entry.title: Re: Feasibility Check for Restricting Visibility of Prici...
entry.content:
Hello,

To ensure that sensitive pricing and PPA (Product and Pricing Agreement) terms like net value are visible only to authorized personnel, it’s critical to set up role-based access controls. This restricts the sales order maker from viewing and modifying sensitive details unless they have specific authorization.

Solution

SAP S/4HANA offers a feature called ‘Role-Based Access to Price Elements,’ Role-Based Access to Price Elements - SAP Community which can help in implementing these security measures. Here is a step-by-step guide:

1. Define Access Levels

  • You can define access levels for price elements such as basic price, taxes, discounts, and PPA terms according to their sensitivity.

2. Assign Access Levels in Pricing Procedure

  • The defined access levels need to be assigned A more detailed look into the setting up of Enhanc… - SAP Community to condition types or subtotal lines in the pricing procedure.

3. Maintain User Authorizations

  • Set up restricted roles for sales order makers and other relevant users using the ‘Maintain Business Roles’ app.
  • Define which elements can be read, which can be modified, and which should be hidden based on user roles.

4. Use the Role-Based Access System

  • This system allows you to display or hide elements on the user interface based on the roles defined. For instance, logistics experts may see only basic pricing, while sales managers can view and alter discounts and surcharges.

Benefits

  • Enhanced Security: Prevents unauthorized access and modifications of sensitive pricing details.
  • Data Control: Allows for proper monitoring and control over pricing data exposure.

By implementing these settings, your organization can effectively manage which users access sensitive pricing information, enhancing data security and integrity.

For more detailed steps on setting these up, please refer to the SAP Help Portal documentation on Role-Based Access to Price Elements.

 

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: How can I get an SAP Certified Development Associate - SAP BTP Extension Developer certificatio
entry.content:
Hi Margit, 

I clicked on the link "SAP Certified Development Associate - SAP BTP Extension Developer" that you shared above, but it is no longer valid.

Can you tell me if SAP has discontinued it or replaced it with another one? If so, can you tell me which one is the current one?

Best Regards,

FAFR

entry.title: Re: WFA on HANA dimension labels
entry.content:
Hi Alex,

You need distinguish if '??' is code or description. For your Department dimension there is only '??' as code which is used when there is no Department assignement. But I see two nodes of '??' for Legal Entity. One is for code '??' and second for code of '17'. It seems you need check your query for Legal Entity hierarchy: It doens't produce any result for INTERNAL_ID of '17'.

Hope this will help you.

Thanks & Regards,

Marian

entry.title: Re: "Help Needed: Identifying Standard CDS View for Sales...
entry.content:
Hello,

To find a CDS view that includes the version field for sales orders (field name: VSNMR_V), you may not find it in the basic I_SalesOrder CDS view. Instead, you should look within other CDS views that are specifically designed for value help or extended purposes. Virtual Data Model and CDS Views - Sales Order

Here are steps you can take to address this requirement:

Using View Browser

  • You can use the ‘View Browser’ Fiori app to search for CDS views that might contain the field ‘VSNMR_V’. In the View Browser, you can search using the field name and check the available fields within each released view.

Check Associated CDS Views

There might be value help CDS views related to sales orders that include additional fields not present in the I_SalesOrder. You can explore views like:

  • I_SalesOrderStdVH

Creating a Custom CDS View

If no standard CDS view contains the field, you can create a custom CDS view:

  1. Custom CDS View App: Use the ‘Custom CDS Views’ app to create a new view. Virtual Data Model and CDS Views - Sales Order (Value Help)
  2. Source Selection: Base your custom view on existing sales order views, but ensure to join or append the field ‘VSNMR_V’ from related tables/views.
  3. Field Inclusion: Explicitly include the ‘VSNMR_V’ field and any other necessary fields.
  4. Publishing and Testing: Publish the custom CDS view and test it to ensure it meets the data requirements.

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

 

Using these steps, you can navigate through SAP S/4HANA Cloud’s CDS views and find or create the necessary data structure.

entry.title: Re: Service broker destination-service-broker failed
entry.content: As SAP note has been expired, can you please share the solution here directly?

entry.title: Re: Could not determine EWM stock data for: Storage Locat...
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

Maybe this Q&A exchange previously done could answer your question and solve your issue :

https://community.sap.com/t5/supply-chain-management-q-a/could-not-determine-ewm-stock-data/qaq-p/9936630

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: Setup External Pricing between Sales Cloud V2 and S/4...
entry.content:
Hello,

To set up External Pricing, you’ll need to ensure proper integration between the SAP Cloud for Customer (C4C) and SAP S/4HANA Cloud. Here are the generalized steps you might consider taking:

  1. Ensure Connectivity:

    • Confirm that the SAP Cloud Connector is reachable and properly configured.

     

  2. Deploy iFlows in SAP Cloud Platform Integration (CPI): SAP CLOUD FOR CUSTOMER INTEGRATION WITH S/4 HANA ON-PREMISE PART II

    • Login to CPI and navigate to the package named ‘SAP Cloud for Customer Integration with SAP S/4HANA’.
    • Identify appropriate iFlows and deploy, such as ’Pricing in Sales Quote Follow Up Scenario in SAP Business Suite’.

     

  3. Code List Mapping in Cloud for Customer:

    • Maintain code list mapping in the Cloud for Customer to ensure synchronization between systems.

     

  4. Set Up Communication Arrangements:

    • Create communication arrangements for various artifacts like Sales Quote Replication, Pricing scenarios, and Sales Order replication.

     

  5. CPI iFlow Configuration

    • Properly configure sender and receiver details for deployment.

     

  6. S/4HANA Configuration (If IDOC Adapter Type is Used):

    • For IDOC adapter types, maintain configurations in transactions WE20 & WE21.

     

  7. Execution Process:

    • Initiate the process from the Cloud for Customer system such as creating a Sales Quote, which triggers external pricing retrieval from S/4HANA and results in sales order creation.

     

By following these steps, you can facilitate accurate external pricing between Sales Cloud V2 and SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, ensuring seamless integration and pricing consistency across both platforms.

For more detailed and situation-specific guidance, consulting the related SAP documentation might be advisable.

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: Set-up EDI connection - 2EL and 4A1
entry.content:
Hello,

When dealing with both scope items 2EL and 4A1, it is indeed possible to configure both, but it requires careful consideration regarding the communication arrangements. Let’s delve into the specifics:

Scope Item 2EL

  • 2EL focuses on the integration of sales with external buyers, 2780447 - Error message ‘No active outbound service could be found’ is raised when setting up Scope Item 2EL / Communication Arrangement SAP_COM_0223 and you will need to set up the communication scenario SAP_COM_0223. This scenario is intended for customers who do not have a connection to SAP Business Network.

 

Scope Item 4A1

  • 4A1, on the other hand, is about automating order-to-invoice processes with SAP Business Network. Sales - Automation of Order-to-Invoice with SAP Business Network (4A1) The setup guide indicates that you need to deactivate SAP_COM_0223 in favor of setting up SAP_COM_0848. This scenario is intended for customers connected via SAP Business Network.
Can Both Scope Items Be Active?

Yes, it is possible to have both running simultaneously. You would set up SAP_COM_0223 for customers without SAP Business Network connections and SAP_COM_0848 for those with connections to SAP Business Network. However, you will need to manage business rules and configurations carefully to ensure that each customer’s transactions route through the correct communication scenario.

Considerations

  • Ensure distinct configurations: Differentiate the setups clearly to avoid conflicts.
  • Monitor integration flows: Regularly check both integrations to ensure data integrity and proper functionality.
  • Support Team Coordination: Engage with SAP support to resolve any integration issues timely.

Thus, while more complex, managing both setups is feasible and allows flexibility depending on your client’s customer needs.

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: MDG SOA - Update Selected Fields
entry.content: Hello Nitin..were you able to figure out how to achieve this requirement, please let me know if yes..

entry.title: Re: SAP CALM API returns forbidden / 403 (in postman)
entry.content:
Hi Friscas,

Are you running a "POST" or a "GET" request using the projects API (https://{region}.alm.cloud.sap/api/calm-projects/v1/projects)?

If you are using a "POST" request, are you providing the necessary JSON body with the details of the new project? If I run a "POST" request but don't provide JSON body with the details of my new project, I get the same 403 error message.

Thanks and regards, 
John

entry.title: Re: sales document type 'LKJ'
entry.content:
Hello,

To use sales document type ‘LKJ’ in Best Practice scope item 55F, you need to ensure that ‘LKJ’ is properly defined in your SAP S/4HANA Cloud system. Here’s how you can proceed:

Steps to Define Sales Document Type ‘LKJ’

  1. Access Configuration for Sales Document Types: Configuration for Sales Scheduling Agreements

    • Navigate to the ‘Manage Your Solution’ app within your SAP S/4HANA Cloud system.
    • Go to the ‘Configuration’ section to manage your sales document types.

  2. Create Sales Document Type ‘LKJ’:

    • If ‘LKJ’ is not yet defined, you will need to create it.
    • Ensure to set the correct configurations according to your business needs. For example, if the JIT (Just-In-Time) delivery is used, ensure that your configurations meet the prerequisites mentioned in related documentation.

  3. Verify Integration with Scope Item 55F:

    • Check the documentation and configuration requirements for scope item 55F to ensure the sales document type ‘LKJ’ aligns well and fulfills the necessary requirements.

  4. Testing and Validation:

    • After defining ‘LKJ’, test it within the context of scope item 55F to confirm it works as expected.
    • Validate that all business processes linked to scope item 55F function correctly with the newly configured sales document type.

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: Option to trigger an event in SAP Cloud ALM for Successful/failed TR import for third-party use
entry.content:
Hello,

We are looking for the availability of an option to trigger an event in SAP Cloud ALM as soon as either

  • A Transport Request is successfully imported (through a Feature), or
  • A Transport Request import fails.

We want to use this event to trigger the next action in a third-party tool. Can you please help to confirm if it's possible in SAP Cloud ALM? If yes, any reference blogs/KBAs will be helpful. 
 
Thanks & Regards


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Billing documents for clearing GLs
entry.content:
In 'Clearing between Company Codes - transaction code BUV' instead of customer and vendor, we are going to use Clearing GLs. 

I would like to know how we can create automated billing documents for the items in those clearing GLs?

entry.title: Re: Option to trigger an event in SAP Cloud ALM for Succe...
entry.content:
SAP Cloud ALM does not currently offer events specific to the import status of Transport Requests in Features. The only available events related to Features are for creation, updating, and deletion of a Feature. These events contain data about the Feature itself, and not the Transport Requests that it is linked to. More Information about these events can be found in the Resource Changes for Features documentation, and information about the event payloads can be found in the Push API Resource Changes - Features documentation.

A Read API for Feature and Transport Request data is planned for Q1 2025, and may be useful in polling Features from the third party software to identify Transport Request statuses. However, this is a "Pull" API and not a "Push" API, so the API request will need to be triggered from an external source (e.g. from the third party software).

If you wish to influence the future development of SAP Cloud ALM and provide direction to our Developers in relation to this topic, please create a request in the SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation campaign on the SAP Customer Influence portal.

entry.title: Yard Logistics: Auto-Generate Opposite Yard Order does not work anymore
entry.content:
Hello, 

I have two problems:

First with the function "Auto-Generate Opposite Yard Order"

I Customized it here: 

My expectation is, that when the Inbound Yard Order is completed (manually or automatically), the system generates automatically an Outbound Yard Order. It worked well until a system update in EWM was made. Now it does not work anymore. I remember, that there is a technical queue in the backroung which must be activated. My guess is, that the queue is not active anymore. Any ideas where I find this queue to check on it? Or any other ideas why it does not work?

Second with "Auto Complete Yard Order"

Customizing:

My expectation here: The Yard Order will be completed automatically, when all Yard Tasks in the Yard Order are activated, started and confirmed. And the Outbound Yard Order will be activated, as soon as it is created. Is my expectation correct? If Yes: It does not work. If No: How is the system behaviour at this point?

 

Many thanks for your help!

Many Greetings,

Sandra

entry.title: Re: Extracting Delta from S/4HANA CDS View to Datasphere...
entry.content: If you have the possibility to download the cds view in excel from the source, then also download the view from datasphere into excel and compare the primary keys. If you find the keys that are missing you might be able to find out if there is a pattern. If you are not able to figure out the problem by yourself open a ticket for CA-DI-IS-ABA.

Thank you for your guidance on how we can resolve this issue.

Best regards,

entry.title: Re: Step-by-Step Guide to SOAP Integration in SAP CPI
entry.content:
Hello, main problem of all is : When you get the "The PayLoad elements cannot fit with the message parts of the BindingOperation" error using SOAP adapter in CPI, most likely the error is in the envelope, you need to remove it from the body, CPI automatically adds it, it would happen that there is 2x there,

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message

def Message processData(Message message) {
def body = message.getBody(String)
def xml = new XmlSlurper().parseText(body)
def newBody = groovy.xml.XmlUtil.serialize(xml.Body.StatusNespolehlivyPlatceRequest)
message.setBody(newBody)
return message
}

Mark

entry.title: Introducing SAP CX Observability (Dynatrace) for SAP Commerce Cloud: A Video Showcase
entry.content:
Are you ready to elevate your SAP Commerce Cloud performance monitoring? In this blog post, I will introduce SAP CX Observability (also known as Dynatrace in SAP Commerce Cloud in the Public Cloud) – the built-in application performance monitoring (APM) tool that empowers you with deep insights and proactive troubleshooting capabilities. To help you get started and master this powerful tool, our team has curated a collection of video tutorials covering everything from basic access and essential monitoring to advanced diagnostics and digital experience management.
(Please note that some videos in this blog post may show UI components that differ slightly from the latest version of the system. Due to unpredictable SaaS updates, it's challenging to ensure every video reflects the most current version. However, the core features and functionalities remain unchanged, so you can safely disregard any minor differences.)

1. Getting Started – Basic Access

Before diving into performance metrics and analytics, it's important to know how to get into Dynatrace. Our introductory video, How to Access Dynatrace – SAP Commerce Cloud, walks you through accessing the tool within your Commerce Cloud environment. This video provides a simple, step-by-step guide to ensure you’re set up and ready to explore further.

2. Essential Monitoring

Once you’re logged in, the next step is to monitor the critical building blocks of your SAP Commerce Cloud Server. In the Essential Monitoring section, a series of videos guide you through monitoring key aspects of your infrastructure and application, including:

  • Hosts Performance – Monitor how your server hosts are performing to catch any issues before they impact user experience. Watch: Monitoring Hosts Performance in Dynatrace for SAP Commerce Cloud
  • Technologies and Processes – Understand how various technologies and background processes are operating, ensuring that your applications run smoothly. Watch: Monitoring Technologies and Processes in Dynatrace for SAP Commerce Cloud
  • Services – Delve into monitoring the performance of services, the most critical component for maintaining high availability and fast response times. Watch: Monitoring Services in Dynatrace for SAP Commerce Cloud
  • Database Monitoring – Keep an eye on your databases to quickly identify and troubleshoot performance bottlenecks. Watch: Database Monitoring in Dynatrace for SAP Commerce Cloud

Each of these videos builds on your knowledge, creating a comprehensive understanding of how to ensure system resilience through proactive monitoring.

3. Additional & Network/Infrastructure Monitoring

A robust monitoring strategy also requires understanding the network and infrastructure that support your SAP Commerce Cloud environment. The Additional Monitoring section focuses on:

  • Host Networking – Gain insights into the network configurations and performance that keep your systems connected. Watch: Host Networking in Dynatrace for SAP Commerce Cloud
  • Smartscape Topology – Learn how to interpret Dynatrace’s Smartscape topology to visualize and troubleshoot dependencies in your environment. Watch: Recognizing the Smartscape Topology in Dynatrace for SAP Commerce Cloud

These videos highlight the importance of monitoring not just the applications themselves, but the underlying infrastructure as well.

4. Dashboard Introduction & Customization

Visualization of data is key to effective monitoring. In this section, videos guide you through the dashboards of Dynatrace:

  • Identifying Dashboards – Discover the default dashboards available in SAP Commerce Cloud and understand how to navigate them. Watch: Identifying Dashboards in Dynatrace for SAP Commerce Cloud
  • E-Commerce Insights Dashboard – Explore a specialized dashboard designed for e-commerce insights, offering an overview of your system’s performance. Watch: Introducing the E-Commerce Insights Dashboard in Dynatrace for SAP Commerce Cloud
  • Creating and Customizing Dashboards – Learn how to tailor dashboards to your specific needs, ensuring you have the right visualizations for your operational requirements. Watch: How to Create and Customize a Dashboard in Dynatrace- SAP Commerce

These tutorials are designed to help you make sense of complex data and create reports that support decision-making.

5. Profiling, Analysis, and Troubleshooting

When issues arise, detailed analysis and troubleshooting are essential. The Profiling, Analysis, and Troubleshooting videos show you how to:

  • Perform Profiling – Identify performance issues with detailed profiling techniques. Watch: Profiling in Dynatrace for SAP Commerce Cloud
  • Continuous Memory Profiling – See how continuous monitoring of memory usage can preempt potential issues. Watch: Introducing Continuous Memory Profiling in Dynatrace for SAP Commerce Cloud
  • Multidimensional Analysis – Use multidimensional analysis to dissect web requests and uncover performance problems. Watch: Analyzing Web Requests with Multidimensional Analysis in Dynatrace for SAP Commerce Cloud
  • Troubleshoot Issues/Problems – Follow a systematic approach to identifying and resolving issues quickly. Watch: Troubleshooting Issues via Dynatrace in SAP Commerce Cloud

These sessions provide you with the tools and techniques to diagnose and resolve problems before they affect your end users.

6. DEM – Digital Experience Management (Paid Services)

Beyond standard monitoring, Dynatrace offers DEM features to further enhance your insights into user behavior. In this section, you'll learn about:

  • Synthetic Monitoring – Understand how synthetic monitoring can simulate user interactions to test your system’s performance. Watch: Understanding Synthetic Monitoring in Dynatrace for SAP Commerce Cloud
  • Real User Monitoring (RUM) – Discover how enabling RUM allows you to capture real-world user experiences, providing invaluable data for refining your systems. Watch: Enabling Real User Monitoring (RUM) in Dynatrace for SAP Commerce Cloud
  • Working with the Frontend – Explore the predefined tool set designed to support Real User Monitoring. Watch: Working with Frontend in Dynatrace for SAP Commerce Cloud
  • Session Segmentation – Understand how session segmentation can help you analyze user behavior on a granular level. Watch: Understanding Session Segmentation of Real User Monitoring in Dynatrace for SAP Commerce Cloud
  • Session Replay – See how session replay offers a detailed view of user interactions, empowering you to troubleshoot and enhance the user experience. Watch: Introducing Session Replay in Dynatrace for SAP Commerce Cloud

These DEM videos elevate your monitoring strategy by focusing not only on technical performance but also on end-user experience.

7. Additional Monitoring Topics

To round out your learning experience, the final set of videos covers additional monitoring topics that are essential for a comprehensive view of your environment:

  • Deployment Monitoring – Learn how to keep track of your SAP Commerce Cloud deployments and ensure continuous performance. Watch: Deployment Monitoring for SAP Commerce Cloud
  • Mobile App Integration – Explore how to connect a Dynatrace subscription to your mobile applications, extending observability beyond the desktop experience. Watch: Connecting a Dynatrace Subscription to a Mobile App in SAP Commerce Cloud

Summary

These video tutorials have been carefully curated to guide you through every aspect of SAP CX Observability with Dynatrace in SAP Commerce Cloud. Whether you're just getting started or looking to troubleshoot and improve the overall user experience, the wealth of knowledge shared in these sessions will empower you to make data-driven decisions and optimize your e-commerce operations.
Dive into the videos, explore each topic area at your own pace, and discover how SAP CX Observability (Dynatrace) can transform your approach to application performance monitoring.
We invite you to share your thoughts, questions, or success stories in the comments below
 
Happy monitoring!

entry.title: Re: Watch & Learn Microlearning Videos – SAP Variant Configuration with CPQ

entry.title: Could not build destination cache key.Isolation strategy 'tenant-user' is used.User undefined in JWT
entry.content:
Hello All,

I developed a CAPM Fiori Application in BAS. I am consuming a Standard SAP API to read the data with the help of destination in CAP service.js. It's running successfully locally. I deployed the changes to DEV and CONS. Deployment is successful but i am not getting data when i access the CAPM application in CAP FLP in DEV and CONS environment and there is no error in FLP console.

When i go to BTP subaccount and check the logs for the same application. I see the below error:

{"messageContext":"destination-cache","package":"connectivity"}, Could not build destination cache key. Isolation strategy 'tenant-user' is used, but tenant id or user id is undefined in JWT.

All the required configurations looks fine.

Kindly help me to resolve this issue.

Regards,

Mahendra

entry.title: Re: Manually editing Prices of a sales quote in SAP Cloud...
entry.content:
Yes, it is possible to manually modify the pricing in a sales quote after an external price request, but it depends on the integration setup and pricing determination logic in SAP Sales Cloud and SAP ERP.

For a more standard setup, follow this link https://community.sap.com/t5/crm-and-cx-q-a/add-manual-price-component-in-c4c-and-then-request-external-price/qaq-p/11414763

However, in our project, we implemented a custom SDK solution to meet specific customer needs.

We introduced a custom price field at the item level in the sales quote and contract, which is controlled via the external pricing procedure in SAP ERP. The ERP pricing procedure defines whether this field is editable or not. This information is then integrated into SAP Sales Cloud and reflected in the "Manually added/changed" column.

Since the standard pricing table is not accessible to users (it was hided), they can only modify the designated editable fields at the item level. Using SDK, we ensured that the manually edited values are transferred from the item table to the pricing table and then sent back to ERP for validation and recalculation via the simulation procedure.

This approach allows for controlled manual price adjustments while maintaining consistency with the external pricing logic in ERP.

entry.title: Re: Inactive Items Not Displayed in User Learning History...
entry.content: yes, but from the user perspective. When I proxy a user and I check his learning history plan. In prod the inactive ones are in the list, but not shown when searched with the search bar. In Test are also in the list but are also shown when are searched in the search bar.

entry.title: Re: 1st SAP Meet-Up of 2025!
entry.content: 👍

entry.title: Re: How can I get an SAP Certified Development Associate - SAP BTP Extension Developer certificatio
entry.content:
Hi @FAFR 
Yes, this is retired.
Please access the SAP Certification Recommendation. This document helps you to understand how your certification will be affected with the upcoming SAP Certification program changes and what you will need to do to stay certified. 
If your certification has been retired, don't worry, you can still maintain your status by taking the recommended alternative certification exam before your current certification expires. Just check the SAP Certification finder tool, and if there is no alternative for your expiring certification, explore the portfolio for learning solutions to keep your skills relevant and your certification current beyond the expiration date.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Need help on FD10N open item warning
entry.content:
Hello,

User is trying to generate a customer report in F.27 but the report which gets generated only has 4 open items which in-turn shows incorrect balances. 

While checking the balances in FD10N I get an warning that "A large number of line items were selected" "Recommended: Only display open items. Do you want to display open items only?"

We have two options for above warning "yes or no". 

When 'Yes' is selected, I can see 39 open items.

When 'No' is selected, I can see only 4 open items. 

What is the reason behind this issue and how can we solve this? 

How can user generate the report with 39 open items which in-turn would be the correct data? 

Kindly help on this issue, Thanks.

entry.title: How to create a DB connection to HANA Data Lake in SLT system.
entry.content:
Hello,

I am trying to add DBCO connection for HANA Datalake in SLT system. But getting certificate issue,

Database error -10709 at CON
Connection failed (RTE:[300015] SSL certificate validation failed: SSL error [536872221]: Unknown error, General error: 0x2000051d | SAPCRYPTOLIB | SSL_connect SSL API error Failed
to verify peer certificate. Peer not trusted. 0xa0600203 |
SSL_ | tls13_handshake Peer not trusted 0xa0600203 | SSL_ |
tls13_msg_decode Peer not trusted 0xa0600203 | SSL_ |
ssl_verify_peer_certificates Peer not trusted 0xa0600203 |
SSL_ | ssl_cert_checker_verify_certificates Peer not
trusted Certificate verification failed 0xa0600203 | SSL_ |
ssl_cert_checker_verify_certificates Peer not trusted -----
BEGIN VERIFICATION RESULT ----- # --- Messages -----------
ERROR: The chain of certificates is incomplete or untrusted,
missing certificate of [C1:AD:77:78] CN=DigiCert SHA2 Secure
Server CA, O=DigiCert Inc, C=US # --- Summary -----------
#01 Certificate (End Entity): VALID Subject:
CN=hanacloud.ondemand.com, O=SAP SE, L=Walldorf,
SP=Baden-WÃ¼rttemberg, C=DE Issuer:
CN=DigiCert SHA2 Secure Server CA, O=DigiCert Inc, C=US
Fingerprint (SHA256):
1B:91:75:BF:EB:78:80:EA:7A:C4:3D:1D:6F:22:29:99:9F:A1:9A:B8:
:2C:9D:C4:13:11:65:6D:7E:DC:28:43 Validity:
Tue Feb 4 19:00:00 2025 / Thu Feb 5 18:59:59 2026 PK
validation: FAILED: Validation of dependents -
Issuer Certificate (Issuer - Only Invalid Certificates
Found) #02 Certificate (Issuer): VALID Subject:
CN=DigiCert SHA2 Secure Server CA, O=DigiCert
Inc, C=US Issuer: CN=DigiCert Global
Root CA, OU=www.digicert.com, O=DigiCert Inc, C=US
Fingerprint (SHA256):
C1:AD:77:78:79:6D:20:BC:A6:5C:88:9A:26:55:02:11:56:52:8B:B6:
:F5:FA:43:E1:B8:E5:A8:3E:3D:2E:AA Validity:
Tue Sep 22 19:00:00 2020 / Sun Sep 22 18:59:59 2030 PK
validation: FAILED: Validation of dependents -
Issuer Certificate (ERROR: Issuer - No Certificates
Found) ----- END VERIFICATION RESULT ----- (ErrCode:
536872221.

below is the connection string we are using.

ENCRYPTION=TLS(TRUSTED_CERTIFICATES=*;TLS_TYPE=rsa;DIRECT=yes);HOST=<SQL endpoint of Datalake>

Am i missing something? or any issue with my connection string?

entry.title: Re: Table KNVI ( Customer Master Tax Indicator ) getting...
entry.content: Did you already look for related change documents on object DEBI with transaction RSSCD100 ?

entry.title: Re: Milestone Billing - SAP SD
entry.content:
Hi Ken! Thanks for that helpful comment. Would you know a workaround for that yet? Since it is not mentioned in the current Roadmap.

We are trying to link the Milestone to the Billing Plan, the function is there at VA01 but its simply not working.

entry.title: How to create a line chart in R3
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I'm looking for a solution on how to create a line chart in R3 system. Are there any classes/interfaces etc. that you can recommend?

Best regards,
Cassandra

entry.title: 900-001-6 error
entry.content: Recruiters are sometimes getting this error when they rae trying to launch a hiring request?. Sometimes, its working fine. What can be the reason?

entry.title: Re: SAP Stammtisch Luzern
entry.content:
Hallo @ivabeg 

Wie versprochen, melde ich mich mit der Info zu unserem Blogbeitrag betreffend TypeScript Entwicklung bei dir. Unsere Erfahrungen hierzu kannst du unter nachfolgendem Link nachlesen. Bei Fragen zu den Inhalten freuen wir uns über deine Nachricht.

https://www.purecons.com/post/getting-started-with-typescript-for-ui5

Liebe Grüsse
Marco

entry.title: EC Promotion - Promote link in worksheet is not available
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I'm facing an issue when it comes to the Promotion link in the Compensation worksheets.

I've followed this information but somehow the link is not available.

Actions for All Plans

Display Settings

 

Permission Roles

The fields Job Family, Job Code, Job Role and PayGrade are included in the form (i've tested with mappings and without mappings and it doesn't work both ways). The fields are set as Read-Only and Reloadable

XML Code to Publish

Regarding the setting in Provisioning, it was probably removed because it's not available.

I couldn't validate the rest of the information because I can't test the publish.

Do you guys have any idea, suggestion?
Am I doing something wrong?

 

Thank you so much for your help

entry.title: Re: IDOC Extension
entry.content: Hi Sandra, One issue we are facing is when we are triggering IDOC through VT02N in WE02 we are getting 2 IDOC generated. One has the standard SHPMNT05 values and the other one has our custom extension values. Checked in NACE also 1 subroutine I created is added and in partner profile also one msg type added in message control. could there be some other reason for 2 idocs generating at same time?

entry.title: Re: unable to drill down in a Crystal report chart
entry.content: Thanks Don - however the issue is happening with the World Sales Report demo file, which isn't connected with the slightly different driver that's in play with the customer's own report.

entry.title: Re: unable to drill down in a Crystal report chart
entry.content: I took a copy of the World Sales Report and checked the drill down from the pie chart and bar chart worked in Crystal Reports 2020 itself (it did) and then saved it back to the platform, where the same problem was still visible. I thinking it must be a problem with a corrupt file somewhere on this particular environment, as it all works fine on a test platform at the same patch level

entry.title: Get certified - Live session series - An additional offering to the self-paced study - Register now
entry.content:
Our Get Certified live session series cover the most relevant topics related to the chosen SAP Certification in a series of knowledge transfer sessions. As a learner, you will receive expert-guided support to complement your self-study activities and prepare for the certification exam. 
The live sessions are an additional offering to the self-paced study, will help you better understand and apply the learned concepts.
During these sessions, you will receive tips and tricks for the exam and learn about the different question types. 

Have we inspired your curiosity - and would you  like to find out more?
Access our Get certified - Live session series for the details.

entry.title: Re: Rating ends with error which indicate wrong Charge wa...
entry.content:
Hello Dominic,

While its hard to give a clear response without checking the actual logic configured on your own SAP CC system, my initial idea is to check if the technical resources are clearly defined on both the Charge Plan responsible for Usage Consumption and also on the CIT when you create them via CIT_UPLOAD, together with the Consumption Item Class.

Furthermore, the trigger periodicity of the Recurring Rate on the other Charge Plan has to be checked, because it is unusual for a Recurring Charge to be triggered from an incoming CIT. Have you specified different Technical Identifiers/Service IDs on the Cross-Catalog-Mapping configuration interface?

Additionally, even thought the Access Denied message comes from the Recurring Rate as stated, it does not mean that the Usage Rate was not taken into account. If both Rates are executed and one leads to a No Access function, the whole Rating will fail, and unfortunately you cannot see that on the Error Message.

As a best practice, it is also advised to avoid the No Access functions on Recurring Rates, unless it is for testing purpose.

Lastly, if nothing works and you are unable to provide screenshots of the solution, you can also try to include both Recurring Charge and Usage Charge on a single Charge Plan, again by keepin in mind the above suggestions, of course, in case that this solution works for your business scenario.

Best,
Xhoi A.

entry.title: In SAP Public Cloud, how to update the currency for cost and budget, which currency should be used?
entry.content:
Dear Community,
In the Import Financial Plan Data app when I want to upload a budget or plant cost using two different currencies eg.: CHF-EUR, the exchange rate is wrong (we get 3500 CHF/3021,57 EUR, that should be 3668,35 EUR)
I tried to modify the exchange rate in the Import foreign rates  app to update the exchange rates, however, this modifications doesn't show in the Import Financial Plan Data app, amounts remain the same.

Financial data plan app might derive the exchange rates from another table.

I also tried to modify the exchange rate type in Central Business Configuration, Maintain category for planning section, exchange rate type from P to M, however, the system doesn't let me to change it.

Any insights or suggestions from your experiences would be greatly appreciated!

Thank you for your support.

entry.title: Re: 0 detection objects selected for processing. - SAP BIS
entry.content: I have reviewed your issue, and I can confirm that the detection method and strategy are working as expected. The detection object data is returning a value of 0, which indicates that no alerts are generated for this detection strategy. If you believe there is still an issue, I recommend connecting with your ABAPer to review the SQL behind this strategy.

entry.title: Re: Generating OAuth Client in SAC, how do we make our te...
entry.content: Hi Martijn, I came to know that if the SAP Analytics Cloud is hosted on SAP Datacenter, we will get the Client Id and Client Secret to be specified manually. If the SAC is hosted on Non-SAP Datacenter, they are automatically generated. But now I am looking for information, token generation functionality varies based on SAP Datacenter or Non-SAP Datacenter Client ID and Secrets.

entry.title: Re: SAP Community - Where are you from?
entry.content: Hello, it is great to meet you. I work in the US outside of Philadelphia. May you check out the Asia Pacific | Location-Based Groups | SAP Community? There are many opportunities to run local events whether topical or casual meet ups. Best of luck!

entry.title: Re: How to pass parameter to the function from XML view
entry.content:
Thanks for the blog.

Can you provide an example where a model parameter is passed to the function, not just a literal?

This doesn't seem to work:

<Button ... press="onPressFun($event , ${uiModel>/something}, 'Saved')"/>

entry.title: Upcoming Webinar | 2025 HR Trends: Turning AI Expectations into Reality
entry.content:
Now that 2025 is well underway, it’s time to take a moment to reflect on the current state of the HR function. With widespread economic volatility, significant technological disruption, and rapidly evolving workforce expectations, HR leaders are having to rethink their strategies to stay one step ahead. One topic, however, continues to make headlines with its promise to change the way people live and work – AI. While there’s no shortage of AI excitement, many HR leaders still face uncertainty around what it truly means for their business and their employees. There’s no question that AI has potential, but how can organizations move beyond hype to make it a reality?

To help cut through the noise so you can unlock real value from AI, we’re thrilled to be hosting a webinar with Kathi Enderes, Senior Vice President Research and Global Industry Analyst at The Josh Bersin Company. Kathi will join Dr. Autumn Krauss, Chief Scientist at SAP SuccessFactors, for an engaging discussion on 2025 HR trends and the impact AI is driving in organizations of all sizes.

During this session, we’ll cover:

  • The latest HR trends and what they mean for your organization
  • Key insights and recommendation for putting AI to work in 2025
  • The priority AI use cases and the measurable impact organizations are realizing

Register here to secure your spot or use the following links to sign up for your local webcast:

  • NA and LATAM time zones: Thursday, April 24, 2025, 11:00 AM EDT
  • APJ and GC time zones: Thursday, April 24, 2025, 1:00 PM SGT
  • EMEA and MEE time zones: Thursday, April 24, 2025, 11:00 AM CEST

We look forward to seeing you there!

entry.content:

https://www.sap.com/dmc/exp/sap-certified-solutions 

 

Best Regards,

Neenu
